The question is asking to identify the leader (premier) that followed each year based on the given data. Since each row represents a year and the corresponding premier, we need to look at the chronological sequence (next year) of the premiers. We can analyze this by examining the "year" and "premiers" columns in sequence.

The question "which leader was in the next year after the group?" is not clear in relation to the provided table. The table lists the premiers (winners) for each year from 2003 to 2013. If you are asking which team became premiers in the year following another specific premier team, we would need more specific details (e.g., "which team was premier in the year after the Port Douglas Crocs?").

However, if the question is asking which premiers appeared in consecutive years, we can see that:
- The Port Douglas Crocs were premiers in both 2004 and 2005 (consecutive years).
- The Centrals Trinity Beach Bulldogs were premiers in 2003 and again in 2007 (not consecutive).
- The Manunda Hawks were premiers in 2006 and again in 2011 (not consecutive).
- The Cairns Saints were premiers in 2008 and again in 2012 (not consecutive).
- The North Cairns Tigers were premiers in 2010 and again in 2013 (not consecutive).
- The South Cairns Cutters were premiers only in 2009 (no consecutive appearance).

Thus, the only premiers who appeared in consecutive years were the Port Douglas Crocs in 2004 and 2005.
